probability_budget.bound: Pr[Split1.IdealAll.MainD(G8(BNR_Adv(A)), SplitC2.RO_Pair(SplitC2.I1.RO, SplitC2.I2.RO)).distinguish () @ &m : res] + Pr[Split1.IdealAll.MainD(G9(BNR_Adv(A)), SplitC2.RO_Pair(SplitC2.I1.RO, SplitC2.I2.RO)).distinguish () @ &m : res] + (Pr[Indist.Distinguish(D(BNR_Adv(A)), IndBlock).game() @ &m : res] - Pr[Indist.Distinguish(D(BNR_Adv(A)), IndRO).game() @ &m : res]) h3: Pr[Split1.IdealAll.MainD(G8(BNR_Adv(A)), SplitC2.RO_Pair(ROin, ROout)).distinguish () @ &m : res] = Pr[CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(BNR_Adv(A)), EncRnd).main() @ &m : res] h41: Pr[Split1.IdealAll.MainD(G9(BNR_Adv(A)), SplitC2.RO_Pair(ROin, ROout)).distinguish () @ &m : res] <= Pr[UFCMA(RO).distinguish() @ &m : res \/ UFCMA.bad2] + Pr[UFCMA(RO).distinguish() @ &m : UFCMA.bad1] hb2: Pr[UFCMA(RO).distinguish() @ &m : res \/ UFCMA.bad2] <= qdec%r * maxr pr_zeropol pr1_poly_out hb1: Pr[UFCMA(RO).distinguish() @ &m : UFCMA.bad1] <= qdec%r * pr1_poly_out ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Pr[CCA_game(BNR_Adv(A), RealOrcls(ChaChaPoly)).main() @ &m : res] <= Pr[CCA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(BNR_Adv(A)), EncRnd).main() @ &m : res] + (Pr[Indist.Distinguish(D(BNR_Adv(A)), IndBlock).game() @ &m : res] - Pr[Indist.Distinguish(D(BNR_Adv(A)), IndRO).game() @ &m : res]) + qdec%r * maxr pr_zeropol pr1_poly_out + qdec%r * pr1_poly_out [438|check]>

budget_ledger.read_as: A factual probability-budget ledger: factor slots are the resources in the product bound, and event obligations are the proof events tied to them. Structure only — no route plan or ranking.

useful_fact_handles.why: Product-bound side conditions often need non-negativity of factors.
Ratio factors in the bound usually need numerator/denominator non-negativity.
Powered ratio factors often need exponent non-negativity closure.
Strict positivity is useful when rewriting ratio-over-itself side conditions.
Strictly positive denominator/numerator facts support positive ratio factors.
Powered positive ratio factors stay positive.
B/B residuals often close by rewriting `divrr` after proving B is nonzero.

probability_budget.read_as: Factual product-budget shape of the goal (bound, factors, event, event-bound bridge). Not a proof script, route ranking, or tactic order.

notes.message: Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: pivot.LHS vs goal.Pr#1 share the wrapper root; diff at arg_1. `apply (<pivot_name> <args>).` is a route candidate with the arg from your goal's module expression at arg_1.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.
ask_manager_for.returns: daemon-verified, committable Pr bridge routes with manager-owned intent payloads

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need pRHL/procedure-equivalence bridge lemma names or context after checking pr_bridge_routes.
ask_manager_for.returns: context-only equiv bridge lemma candidates (names to look up/apply); not a verified Pr route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need local lemma hints before choosing a Pr-level proof route.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and proof-route context

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need exact byphoare/phoare-loop tactic syntax before probing the probability route.

items.label: Before inline expansion #5
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #5 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
